name: The Housing Council At Pathstone, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor serving Monroe County since 1971, offering free foreclosure prevention, tenant rights education, and homebuyer counseling to 8,000+ households annually.
description_long:
The Housing Council at PathStone is a long-established non-profit organization that has been providing housing-related services in Monroe County and surrounding areas for over 50 years, since its founding in 1971. The organization focuses exclusively on serving low- to moderate-income residents through evidence-based counseling and direct assistance programs.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of housing services across three primary client groups: homeowners, prospective homebuyers, and tenants. For homeowners, they provide foreclosure prevention counseling, homeowner repair grants information, and property improvement guidance. First-time homebuyers receive pre-purchase education, one-on-one counseling sessions, and access to their eHome Online platform. Tenants receive guidance on housing discrimination issues, apartment-finding resources, and free educational materials about housing rights and responsibilities. They also serve landlords with property management training, DHS inspection scheduling assistance, and rental property operation resources.

The Housing Council distinguishes itself through its multi-stakeholder approach—simultaneously serving homeowners, tenants, and landlords within the same community ecosystem. Their Housing Hotline operates during business hours (9 AM–Noon) for immediate access to counselors. The organization has maintained continuous operation since 1971 and serves over 8,000 households annually, indicating significant community trust and established infrastructure. They operate through a variety of programs including the Making Moves Program and Rochester FEC initiatives.

As a non-profit with HUD-aligned services, The Housing Council at PathStone appears to operate with genuine mission-driven intent focused on housing stability rather than profit generation. However, the website provides limited detail about specific outcomes, counselor certifications, or measurable success rates. Service availability appears geographically limited to Monroe County and surrounding areas, and the website content emphasizes program names without detailed descriptions of counseling methodologies or eligibility criteria.
pros: Established 50+ year track record serving 8,000+ households annually in Monroe County
Completely free services with no fees or hidden costs for counseling
Multi-specialized approach serving homeowners, first-time buyers, tenants, and landlords
Housing Hotline provides direct phone access to counselors during business hours
Offers both one-on-one counseling and online education platforms (eHome Online)
Addresses housing discrimination issues and tenant rights education
Provides foreclosure prevention counseling to help homeowners avoid losing homes
HUD-aligned services suggest compliance with federal standards for housing counseling
cons: Limited service area restricted to Monroe County and surrounding regions only
Website lacks specific information about counselor certifications or HUD approval status
No published data on success rates, outcomes, or client testimonials available
Housing Hotline operates only 3 hours daily (9 AM–Noon), limiting accessibility
Website provides program names but sparse details on actual counseling methodologies or eligibility requirements
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Monroe County seeking free pre-purchase education and one-on-one guidance
Homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking property improvement assistance in the region
Tenants needing guidance on housing discrimination, rights, and apartment resources
Low- to moderate-income households unable to afford private housing counseling services
diagnosis: The Housing Council at PathStone is best for low- to moderate-income residents in Monroe County, New York seeking completely free, mission-driven housing counseling and education without fees. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Monroe County and surrounding areas, plus limited daily availability (9 AM–Noon hotline hours), which may not accommodate all working households seeking immediate assistance.
services: Foreclosure prevention counseling for at-risk homeowners
First-time homebuyer pre-purchase education and counseling
Homeowner repair grants information and assistance
Tenant housing rights education and resources
Apartment finding and rental search assistance
DHS inspection scheduling assistance for rental properties
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
